evals.prompt: I want to design the team invitation feature. The PRD is at plans/team-invitations/prd.md covering inviting members, role assignment, and revoking invites.
evals.expected_output: Reads prd.md first and acknowledges its contents. Explores the codebase for existing UI components, design system, and navigation patterns. Conducts a structured interview covering all 8 domains (IA, user flows, screen inventory, component mapping, interactions & responsive, accessibility, content & copy, design references) one question at a time using AskUserQuestion with recommendations. Writes standalone spec.md into plans/team-invitations/ with: step-by-step user flows for each story (invite, assign role, revoke) with mermaid diagrams for branching flows, screen inventory with full state tables (empty/loading/default/error/permission-denied/partial), component inventory mapped to existing codebase, responsive breakpoint table, accessibility by concern (focus/keyboard/screen reader/visual), and content table with exact strings. Updates pipeline.md Decisions Log and Status. Should NOT include data models, API endpoints, or architecture. Should end by suggesting '/engineering'.

evals.prompt: Let's design the dashboard sharing feature. Users need to share dashboards externally via links with granular visibility controls. No PRD exists yet.
evals.expected_output: Checks for prd.md (finds none) and notes that defined requirements produce better specs but proceeds if user wants. Explores codebase for existing sharing patterns, link generation UI, permission controls, and design system. Conducts full 8-domain interview using AskUserQuestion. Should produce standalone spec.md with: IA for share entry points, user flows with mermaid diagrams (share creation flow has branches for permission levels), screen inventory with state tables (no shares/active shares/expired links), responsive breakpoint table, accessibility per interaction, content table. Flags PRD gaps in PRD Addendum section. Should NOT specify auth token storage, API design, or database models. Should end by suggesting '/engineering'.

evals.prompt: design: The PRD at plans/notifications/prd.md is missing user stories for bulk actions, the digest email flow, the mute flow, and the notification preferences screen. Let's design anyway.
evals.expected_output: Detects 4 missing user stories from prd.md. Notes each as an assumption, continues the full interview, and flags all 4 in the PRD Addendum section as 'BLOCKING — backport to PRD before engineering.' Should complete the full spec.md despite gaps. Should produce standalone spec.md covering the missing stories based on reasonable assumptions with all required sections.

evals.prompt: design: The pipeline.md at plans/permissions/ has rollback notes saying 'admin vs super-admin role management contradiction found during engineering.' The spec.md exists with most domains resolved.
evals.expected_output: Reads pipeline.md Rollback Notes for trigger and affected domains. Resumes only affected domains — does NOT re-interview resolved decisions. After resolving, updates spec.md, clears Rollback Notes in pipeline.md, and directs user back to /engineering.

evals.prompt: design: The prd.md at plans/permissions/ says 'admins can manage all roles' but scope.md says 'only super-admins can assign admin roles.' These contradict each other.
evals.expected_output: Detects contradictory requirements (admin vs super-admin role management). Appends to ## Rollback Notes in pipeline.md with the contradiction as trigger, affected domains, and any existing design decisions to preserve. Rolls back to /product rather than resolving the contradiction inline. Should NOT continue the design interview past this point.
expectations: Rollback notes mention the admin vs super-admin contradiction
Does not continue the design interview after detecting the contradiction

evals.prompt: design: We're adding AI search to replace keyword search. The PRD is at plans/ai-search/prd.md. The app uses React with shadcn/ui.
evals.expected_output: Reads prd.md and explores codebase for existing search UI and shadcn/ui components. Conducts full 8-domain interview. Runs quality gate after all domains resolved. Writes standalone spec.md with: user flows including mermaid diagram for search flow (has branches for suggestions, no results, errors), screen inventory with state tables (5+ states per screen including empty query, loading, results, no results, error, permission-denied), component mapping to shadcn/ui (Command/Combobox for input, Card for results, Skeleton for loading), responsive breakpoint table (desktop/tablet/mobile), accessibility by 4 concerns, content table with all strings. Should reuse shadcn/ui components by name. Should NOT specify embedding models, vector databases, or API design. Should suggest '/engineering'.

evals.prompt: design: I need to design a simple settings toggle for dark mode. PRD is at plans/dark-mode/prd.md with one user story: 'As a user I can toggle dark mode.'
evals.expected_output: Reads prd.md. Despite simplicity, conducts full 8-domain interview since design is required for all features. Produces standalone spec.md with: IA (settings location), flow (single toggle — may not need mermaid since no 2+ branches), screen inventory with state table, component mapping (toggle/switch from design system), interaction spec with feedback behavior, responsive breakpoints, accessibility (toggle label, state announcement, contrast in both modes, reduced motion), content table (toggle label, confirmation if any). Quality gate should pass — every story mapped, states defined, a11y covered. Suggests '/engineering'.

evals.prompt: design: Resume designing the checkout flow. plans/checkout/spec.md has progress markers showing domains 1-4 resolved. pipeline.md has no rollback notes.
evals.expected_output: Reads spec.md and detects <!-- progress: domain-N resolved --> markers for domains 1-4 (IA, user flows, screen inventory, component mapping). Skips those domains entirely. Resumes interview at domain 5 (interactions & responsive). Completes domains 5-8, runs quality gate, then writes final spec.md removing all progress markers. Updates pipeline.md.
expectations: Does not ask questions about domains 1-4 (IA, flows, screens, components)
First interview question is about domain 5 (interactions/responsive) or later
Final spec.md contains no <!-- progress: --> markers
Updates pipeline.md Status and Decisions Log
